blocks.heading: Yes / No Questions
blocks.body:
• Yes / No Questions take a yes or no answer.
  • An auxiliary verb comes before the subject.
  • Structure: Auxiliary + Subject + Main Verb +...?

blocks.heading: Wh-Questions
blocks.body:
• Wh-Questions start with who, what, where, when, why, how.
  • Word order after the wh-word matters.
  • Start with the most common patterns.

blocks.heading: Alternative Questions
blocks.body:
• Alternative Questions offer a choice with or.
  • The structure follows a general question.
  • The answer is one of the options.

blocks.heading: Topics in This Section
blocks.body:
TopicLevelDescription
Yes / No QuestionsA1Questions with yes / no answers
Wh-QuestionsA1–A2Questions with question words
Answers to QuestionsA1–A2Short and full answers
Alternative QuestionsA2Questions with a choice
Question TagsB1Question tags
Negative QuestionsB1Negative questions

blocks.heading: Question Tags
blocks.body:
• Question Tags are short questions at the end of a sentence.
  • The rule is positive sentence + negative tag, or the other way round.
  • Use them to check or confirm information.

blocks.heading: Study Tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with Yes / No Questions and Wh-Questions.

Then add the other question types.
